growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: minimal salts medium (reduced) + 40 mM sodium butyrate, N2-CO2 atmosphere

up.method: HPLC (ion-exclusion column); 42 mM butyrate decreased to 10 mM over 8 days (192 h) => 32 mM/192 h = 0.167 mmol/L/h volumetric average, NOT per gDW

sec.method: HPLC; 32 mM butyrate converted to 59 mM acetate; 59 mM/192 h = 0.307 mmol/L/h volumetric average, NOT per gDW
produced by butyrate oxidation but immediately consumed by methanogen; only trace <43 umol/L headspace detected (interspecies H2 transfer)

product_yields.acetate_per_butyrate: 59 mM acetate / 32 mM butyrate = 1.84 mol/mol (theoretical 2.0 per 2 butyrate->4 acetate)

growth_records.cite: Enoki et al. Comparative Proteomic Analysis of Methanothermobacter themautotrophicus ΔH in Pure Culture and in Co-Culture with a Butyrate-Oxidizing Bacterium. PLoS ONE (2011). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0024309. [PMC3164167]
growth_records.snippet: Butyrate consumption began after 9 days of inoculation, and 42 mM of initial substrate decreased to 10 mM in another 8 days... this coculture finally, almost stoichiometrically, converted 32 mM of butyrate to 59 mM of acetate and 14 mM of m
growth_records.notes: CO-CULTURE (butyrate-oxidizing partner). Butyrate uptake = (42-10) mM / 8 days = 32 mM/192 h = 0.167 mmol/L/h (42->10 mM and 8-day window explicit). Acetate secretion = 59 mM / 192 h = 0.307 mmol/L/h (59 mM explicit; using the same 8-day oxidation window; concentration itself is directly measured). H2 produced but scavenged (<43 umol/L headspace). Own growth rate NOT separately reported (only methanogen generation time of 19 h given, from methane); at syntrophic steady state the two partners are growth-coupled. No biomass reported; volumetric rates only. Overall reaction stated: 2 butyrate + CO2 + 2 H2O = 4 acetate + CH4. Temperature 55 C, anaerobic, unstirred.

carbon_growth.medium: minimal salts medium (reduced) + 40 mM sodium butyrate, N2-CO2 atmosphere
carbon_growth.citation: Enoki et al. Comparative Proteomic Analysis of Methanothermobacter themautotrophicus ΔH in Pure Culture and in Co-Culture with a Butyrate-Oxidizing Bacterium. PLoS ONE (2011). DOI:
